2605.03440.title: A Comparison of Traditional Machine Learning Algorithms and LSTM-Based Deep Learning Models for Email Sentiment Analysis
2605.03440.paper_summary: The rapid growth of electronic communication has necessitated more robust systems for email classification and sentiment detection. This study presents a comparative performance analysis between traditional machine learning algorithms and deep learning architectures, specifically focusing on Support Vector Machines (SVMs), Logistic Regression, Naive Bayes, and Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM). Utilizing Word2Vec embeddings for feature representation, our experimental results indicate that the SVM model with a linear kernel achieves the highest efficiency and accuracy, reaching a peak performance of 98.74%. While the LSTM model demonstrates exceptional recall capabilities in detecting spam-related sentiments, it requires significantly more computational time compared to discriminative statistical models. Detailed evaluations via confusion matrices further reveal that traditional classifiers remain highly robust for dense vector spaces. This research concludes that for email detection tasks, SVM offers the most optimal balance between predictive precision and processing speed. These findings provide critical insights for developing high-performance automated email filtering systems in professional and academic environments.
2605.03440.paper_summary_zh: 電子通信的快速增長促使了對電子郵件分類和情感檢測更強大系統的需求。這項研究呈現了傳統機器學習算法與深度學習架構之間的比較性能分析，特別關注支持向量機（SVM）、邏輯回歸、朴素貝葉斯和長短期記憶（LSTM）。利用Word2Vec嵌入進行特徵表示，我們的實驗結果表明，具有線性核的SVM模型達到了最高的效率和準確性，達到98.74%的峰值性能。雖然LSTM模型在檢測垃圾郵件相關情感方面顯示出卓越的召回能力，但與判別統計模型相比，它需要顯著更多的計算時間。通過混淆矩陣的詳細評估進一步揭示，傳統分類器在密集向量空間中仍然非常穩健。本研究得出結論，對於電子郵件檢測任務，SVM提供了預測精度和處理速度之間的最佳平衡。這些發現為在專業和學術環境中開發高性能自動電子郵件過濾系統提供了重要的見解。
2605.03440.author: Virdio Samuel Saragih et.al.
2605.03440.authors: Virdio Samuel Saragih, Baruna Abirawa, Kartini Lovian Simbolon, Luluk Muthoharoh, Ardika Satria, Martin C. T. Manullang
